steps.title: Spot the defining contrast
steps.content: Alexander "was at work almost constantly" while he "avoided the clubs and usually dined alone" — his entire schedule was filled by work, with no social breaks. The word must mean without stopping or without interruption.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean working in an uninterrupted, ongoing way. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — imperceptibly: "was at work almost imperceptibly" — imperceptibly means in a way too slight to notice. The passage is not describing hidden or invisible work. ✗
  • Choice B — fearfully: "was at work almost fearfully" — fearfully means with fear or anxiety. No emotional state is described in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — continuously: "was at work almost continuously" — continuously means without stopping, matching the sense that his schedule was entirely consumed by work. ✓
  • Choice D — hastily: "was at work almost hastily" — hastily means with excessive speed or hurry. The passage emphasizes duration and dedication, not speed. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
